title: Rising Costs: How Global Events and Energy Prices Shape Daily Life
subtitle: From the price of a plane ticket to the cost of living, energy instability is changing the way Americans live and work.

summary.en: Rising energy costs are putting significant pressure on American families and businesses. The conflict in Iran has caused jet fuel prices to double, leading to higher fees and reduced services for air travelers. Meanwhile, some experts argue that moving toward green energy could actually help lower the cost of living for working-class people. Political groups are now pushing for policies that connect climate action with economic stability. As the economy faces these challenges, many citizens are left wondering how these shifts will affect their future.
summary.zh: 能源成本上漲正對美國家庭與企業造成巨大壓力。伊朗的衝突導致航空燃油價格翻倍，進而推高了費用並縮減了航空旅客的服務。與此同時，一些專家主張，轉向綠色能源實際上可能有助於降低勞工階級的生活成本。政治團體目前正推動將氣候行動與經濟穩定連結起來的政策。隨著經濟面臨這些挑戰，許多公民不禁質疑這些轉變將如何影響他們的未來。

paragraphs.en: Across the United States, families are feeling the weight of a difficult economic climate. From the rising price of gasoline to increasing mortgage rates, many people are finding it harder to pay for their daily needs. According to NPR, the job market has remained stagnant, leaving many households under significant financial pressure as they try to make ends meet.
paragraphs.zh: 在美國各地，家庭正感受到艱困經濟環境帶來的壓力。從汽油價格上漲到房貸利率攀升，許多人發現要負擔日常需求變得更加困難。根據美國公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，就業市場持續停滯，導致許多家庭在試圖維持生計時，承受著巨大的財務壓力。

paragraphs.en: One of the most visible signs of this economic strain is found in the travel industry. The ongoing conflict in Iran has caused the price of jet fuel to rise sharply, nearly doubling since the start of the year. This has created a difficult situation for airlines, which must manage these high costs while keeping ticket prices attractive to customers. Nick Ewen, editor in chief of The Points Guy, noted to NPR that fuel is usually an airline's second-largest expense after labor. Because consumers are sensitive to price changes, airlines cannot simply pass all these costs on to passengers through higher ticket prices. Instead, many companies are finding other ways to increase revenue.
paragraphs.zh: 這種經濟緊縮最明顯的跡象之一出現在旅遊業。伊朗持續發生的衝突導致航空燃油價格急劇飆升，自今年年初以來幾乎翻了一倍。這為航空公司帶來了艱難的處境，他們必須在管理這些高昂成本的同時，又要讓票價對顧客保持吸引力。旅遊資訊網站 The Points Guy 的總編輯尤恩（Nick Ewen）向 NPR 指出，燃油通常是航空公司繼人力成本之後的第二大支出。由於消費者對價格變動相當敏感，航空公司無法單純透過提高票價將所有成本轉嫁給乘客。因此，許多公司正設法尋找其他增加營收的管道。

paragraphs.en: These changes are already affecting travelers. Major U.S. carriers, including American, Alaska, Delta, Southwest, and United, have all increased their checked baggage fees by about $10 per bag. Additionally, some airlines are reducing services to save money. For example, Delta announced that it will stop providing snacks and drinks on flights shorter than 350 miles starting in May. These adjustments follow the recent bankruptcy and shutdown of Spirit Airlines, which struggled to survive as fuel costs climbed. Experts warn that even if the conflict in the region ends soon, it could take months for fuel supplies to return to normal and for prices to stabilize.
paragraphs.zh: 這些變動已經影響到旅客。包括美國航空、阿拉斯加航空、達美航空、西南航空和聯合航空在內的主要美國航空公司，都將託運行李費用調漲了約 10 美元。此外，部分航空公司為了節省開支正在縮減服務。例如，達美航空宣布從 5 月開始，將停止在飛行距離短於 350 英里的航班上提供零食與飲料。這些調整是在精神航空（Spirit Airlines）近期破產倒閉後所做出的，該公司在燃油成本攀升的壓力下難以生存。專家警告，即便該地區的衝突很快結束，燃油供應恢復正常並使價格穩定下來，可能仍需數個月的時間。

paragraphs.en: While many people focus on these immediate financial problems, some experts are looking for long-term solutions. A group of progressive thinkers is suggesting that the answer to the cost-of-living crisis might be found in climate policy. A new report from the Climate and Community Institute (CCI) argues that the climate crisis is a major cause of economic instability. Instead of viewing environmental goals as a burden, the group suggests that moving toward green energy can be a tool to make life more affordable for working people.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管許多人將目光集中在這些迫在眉睫的財務問題上，一些專家正尋求長期解決方案。一群進步派思想家認為，生活成本危機的答案或許能從氣候政策中找到。氣候與社區研究所（Climate and Community Institute, CCI）的一份新報告指出，氣候危機是造成經濟不穩定的主要原因。該組織認為，與其將環境目標視為負擔，不如將轉向綠色能源視為一種能讓勞工階級生活更負擔得起的工具。

paragraphs.en: This idea, which the CCI calls “green economic populism,” aims to challenge the belief that climate action makes life more expensive. According to The Guardian, the group recently shared data showing that a large majority of voters—including 65% of Republicans—believe that taking action on climate change could help lower the cost of living. Patrick Bigger, a research director at the CCI, stated that the government has failed to invest enough in working people over the last few decades, leading to the pain families feel today. The group hopes to work with unions and social movements to create policies that focus on economic fairness rather than just the profits of large corporations.
paragraphs.zh: 這個被 CCI 稱為「綠色經濟民粹主義」的理念，旨在挑戰「氣候行動會讓生活變得更昂貴」的觀點。根據《衛報》報導，該組織近期分享的數據顯示，絕大多數選民（包括 65% 的共和黨人）認為，針對氣候變遷採取行動有助於降低生活成本。CCI 的研究主任比格（Patrick Bigger）表示，政府在過去幾十年未能對勞工階級進行足夠的投資，這才導致了家庭今日所感受到的痛苦。該組織希望與工會及社會運動合作，制定出不僅關注大企業利潤，更聚焦於經濟公平的政策。

paragraphs.en: This approach is not entirely new. It builds on the framework of the Green New Deal, which was popularized in 2018. That movement sought to connect the need for a cleaner environment with the need for better jobs, housing, and healthcare. Supporters believe that by changing the way the economy is built, the country can solve both the climate crisis and the cost-of-living crisis at the same time. Naomi Klein, a prominent author and member of the CCI advisory board, noted that this strategy is designed to prove that reducing emissions does not have to make life harder for the average person.
paragraphs.zh: 這種做法並非全然創新，它是建立在 2018 年普及的「綠色新政（Green New Deal）」架構之上。該運動試圖將對更乾淨環境的需求，與對更好工作、住房及醫療保健的需求連結起來。支持者相信，透過改變經濟的建構方式，國家可以同時解決氣候危機與生活成本危機。身為知名作家及 CCI 諮詢委員會成員的克萊恩（Naomi Klein）指出，這項策略旨在證明，減少碳排放並不必然會讓一般人的生活變得更艱難。

paragraphs.en: As the country looks toward the future, the debate over how to handle these rising costs continues. Whether through immediate changes in the travel industry or long-term shifts in energy policy, it is clear that the economy is in a period of transition. For now, many Americans are watching closely to see how these developments will influence their financial security and their decisions in upcoming elections. With uncertainty remaining in both global energy markets and domestic policy, the path forward remains a subject of intense discussion.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著國家展望未來，關於如何處理這些上漲成本的辯論仍在持續。無論是透過旅遊業的即時調整，還是能源政策的長期轉型，顯而易見的是，經濟正處於過渡期。目前，許多美國人正密切關注這些發展將如何影響他們的財務安全，以及他們在即將到來的選舉中會做出何種決定。由於全球能源市場與國內政策仍存在不確定性，未來的路徑依然是各界激烈討論的焦點。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The burden or pressure caused by a difficult situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（重量），這裡比喻經濟上的壓力。文中：Across the United States, families are feeling the weight of a difficult economic climate.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have just enough money to pay for the things that you need.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 勉強維持生計、收支平衡。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個固定慣用語，無法從字面拆解意思。文中：leaving many households under significant financial pressure as they try to make ends meet.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To transfer a cost to someone else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（通過、傳遞），這裡指將成本轉嫁給他人。文中：airlines cannot simply pass all these costs on to passengers through higher ticket prices.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that is difficult to deal with or that causes worry.
keyTerms.noteZh: 在經濟語境下指造成財務困難的因素。文中：Instead of viewing environmental goals as a burden, the group suggests that moving toward green energy can be a tool to make life more affordable for working people.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: As household bills soar, is it time for a ‘working-class climate agenda’?

sources.name: NPR Business
sources.title: If you are struggling to make ends meet, NPR wants to hear from you

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: The Iran war sent jet fuel prices sky-high. Here's what air travelers should know

quiz.question: What specific change has Delta announced in response to rising operational costs?

options.A: They will increase ticket prices for all domestic flights by $10.
options.B: They will eliminate complimentary snacks and drinks on flights under 350 miles.
options.C: They will stop offering checked baggage services on short-haul flights.
options.D: They will reduce the number of flights scheduled for routes under 350 miles.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Delta 明確宣布從五月開始，在 350 英里以下的航班上停止提供零食與飲料。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到航空公司無法直接將所有成本轉嫁到票價上；選項 C 錯誤，因為航空公司是增加行李費而非取消服務；選項 D 在文中並未提及。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might fuel prices remain high even if the conflict in Iran ends?

options.A: Because airlines have already signed long-term contracts for expensive fuel.
options.B: Because the global demand for jet fuel is expected to rise sharply.
options.C: Because the supply chain requires significant time to recover and stabilize.
options.D: Because the U.S. government plans to impose new taxes on fuel imports.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第三段最後提到，專家警告即使衝突結束，燃料供應恢復正常並穩定價格仍需數月時間，這暗示了供應鏈恢復的延遲性。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 B 與文中提到的「需求」無關，重點在於「供應」；選項 D 為無中生有的資訊。

quiz.question: In the context of the first paragraph, what does the phrase "make ends meet" mean?

options.A: To finish a task or project before a deadline.
options.B: To earn enough money to cover basic living expenses.
options.C: To connect different parts of an economic plan.
options.D: To reach an agreement between two opposing groups.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。「Make ends meet」是一個慣用語，意指「收支平衡」或「勉強維持生計」。文中提到人們在經濟壓力下努力維持生活，符合此定義。選項 A、C、D 皆與該片語在經濟上下文中的意思完全無關，屬於錯誤的字面解釋或臆測。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the current economic situation?

options.A: The travel industry is solely responsible for the current cost-of-living crisis.
options.B: Green energy policies are the only way to immediately lower gasoline prices.
options.C: Americans are facing economic challenges, leading to both immediate corporate adjustments and debates over long-term policy solutions.
options.D: The Green New Deal has already successfully solved the financial problems of most American families.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文首先描述了經濟困境對旅遊業的短期影響（如漲價、減服務），隨後探討了學界對於透過氣候政策解決經濟問題的長遠看法，總結了當前的過渡時期。選項 A 過於偏頗；選項 B 文中未說這是「唯一」方法；選項 D 錯誤，因為 Green New Deal 仍是討論中的框架，而非已成功的解決方案。
